{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "HR Manager",
  "description": "J.A.K Group is a Global Player who is Focused &amp; Growth Driven, committed to meeting Emerging Energy Needs through Ethical Practices, applies Forward Thinking Innovative Solutions in Creating Value, Whilst being at the Forefront of the Energy Transition.J.A.K Groupis committed to turning Energy into Opportunity and Building an Energy Business for the Future. We are Dynamic, Responsive, Ethically Responsible, Proactive and Growing. We Believe in Creating Values and Making a Difference.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Manager

Job Location: Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities


	Manage recruitment and onboarding
	Develop and implement HRpolicies
	Handle employee relations and performance management.
	Coordinate staff training, development, and career growth programs
	Manage compensation, benefits, and payroll coordination.
	Prepare HR reports and maintain accurate employee records.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws, company policies, and HR best practices.


Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in HR, Business Administration, or related fields
	7+ years of HR experience
	HR certification is an advantage.


Required Skills:


	Communication and interpersonal skills
	Leadership and problem-solving abilities
	Recruitment and employee management
	Knowledge of HR practices and labor laws
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office/HR software.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Aplication&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: cv@jakgroup.ng and legal@jakgroup.ng using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

HR Manager Job at J.A.K Group




        
    

        
        J.A.K Group is a Global Player who is Focused & Growth Driven, committed to meeting Emerging Energy Needs through Ethical Practices, applies Forward Thinking Innovative Solutions in Creating Value, Whilst being at the Forefront of the Energy Transition.J.A.K Groupis committed to turning Energy into Opportunity and Building an Energy Business for the Future. We are Dynamic, Responsive, Ethically Responsible, Proactive and Growing. We Believe in Creating Values and Making a Difference.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: HR Manager

Job Location: Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Manage recruitment and onboarding
	Develop and implement HRpolicies
	Handle employee relations and performance management.
	Coordinate staff training, development, and career growth programs
	Manage compensation, benefits, and payroll coordination.
	Prepare HR reports and maintain accurate employee records.
	Ensure compliance with labor laws, company policies, and HR best practices.


Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in HR, Business Administration, or related fields
	7+ years of HR experience
	HR certification is an advantage.


Required Skills:


	Communication and interpersonal skills
	Leadership and problem-solving abilities
	Recruitment and employee management
	Knowledge of HR practices and labor laws
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office/HR software.


Salary
N250,000 - N300,000 monthly.

Method of Aplication 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] and [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Graphic Designer",
  "description": "Proposed City University, Abuja, is currently recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below:&nbsp;

Job Position: Graphic Designer
Job Location: Central Business District, Abuja (FCT)&nbsp;
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a creative, technically skilled, and brand-conscious Graphic Designer to join the founding team of a new private university in Abuja.
	This is an exciting opportunity to shape the visual identity of a world-class institution from the ground up.
	You will be responsible for producing high-quality design assets across print, digital, and environmental media &ndash; from brochures and billboards to social media graphics and event backdrops.
	Your work will directly influence how prospective students, parents, donors, and partners perceive the university.
	This is not just a design role; it is a chance to build a brand legacy.


Key Responsibilities
Brand &amp; Creative Development:


	Develop and maintain the university&rsquo;s visual identity (logos, colour palettes, typography, imagery guidelines) in line with the brand strategy.
	Create a comprehensive brand style guide and ensure consistent application across all departments.
	Conceptualise and execute creative campaigns for student recruitment, Open Days, convocation ceremonies, and fundraising events.


Design Production:


	Design print collateral: prospectuses, flyers, posters, banners, brochures, magazines, letterheads, and business cards.
	Design digital assets: website graphics, social media posts (Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook), email newsletters, digital ads, and presentations (PowerPoint/Canva/Google Slides).
	Produce environmental graphics: signage, wayfinding, exhibition stands, pull up banners, and campus interior displays.
	Edit and retouch photographs; source or create illustrations, icons, and infographics.


Collaboration &amp; Project Management:


	Work closely with the Marketing, Academic Affairs, and Student Affairs teams to translate their content into compelling visuals.
	Manage multiple design projects simultaneously, from brief to final delivery, meeting tight deadlines (e.g., accreditation documents, admission cycles).
	Liaise with external printers, sign makers, and vendors to ensure high-quality production within budget.


Digital &amp; Multimedia Support:


	Assist in basic video editing (e.g., short promotional clips for social media) and animation (motion graphics) when required.
	Ensure all designs are optimised for web and mobile (file size, resolution, accessibility).
	Brand Compliance
	Review all departmental materials to ensure visual consistency and brand integrity.
	Maintain an organised digital asset library (logos, photos, templates) accessible to authorised staff.


Qualifications &amp; Experience
Essential:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Graphic Design, Visual Arts, Fine Arts, Communication Design, or a closely related field.
	Minimum of 2&ndash;5 years of professional graphic design experience in a corporate, agency, or institutional setting (experience in education, publishing, or non-profit sectors is a strong plus).


A strong portfolio demonstrating:


	Brand identity projects
	Print and digital collateral
	Typography and layout skills
	Understanding of colour theory and composition
	Proven proficiency in industry-standard software: Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) and optionally Adobe Premiere Pro / After Effects.
	Experience preparing print-ready files and liaising with commercial printers.


Salary &amp; Benefits


	A monthly salary of N200,000 - N300,000 monthly depending on experience.
	Access to university facilities (library, sports, etc.)
	Opportunities for professional development (training, conferences, software subscriptions).


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send a detailed CV to: citybiz.infotech.school@gmail.com using &ldquo;Graphic Designer &ndash; [Your Full Name]&rdquo; as the subject of the email.

Note


	A cover letter (1&ndash;2 pages) addressing:
	Your design philosophy and approach to brand building
	A specific example of a design project that improved brand perception or engagement metrics
	A link to your online portfolio (Behance, Dribbble, personal website) or attach a PDF portfolio showing 5&ndash;12 of your best projects with brief descriptions.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-05-25",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-13",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",
  "hiringOrganization": {
    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Proposed City University"
    
  },
  "jobLocation": {
    "@type": "Place",
    "address": {
      "@type": "PostalAddress",
       "postalCode": ". ",
       "addressRegion": ". ",
       "streetAddress": ". ",
      "addressLocality": "Abuja",
      "addressCountry": "NG"
     
      
    }
  },
  "identifier": {

    "name": "",
    "value": "199412"
  },
  "baseSalary": {

    "currency": "NGN",
    "value": {
      "@type": "QuantitativeValue",
      "minValue": 150000,
      "maxValue": 250000,
      "unitText": "MONTH"
    }
  }
}



Graphic Designer Job at Proposed City University




        
    

        
        Proposed City University, Abuja, is currently recruiting suitably qualified candidates to fill the position below: 

Job Position: Graphic Designer
Job Location: Central Business District, Abuja (FCT) 
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a creative, technically skilled, and brand-conscious Graphic Designer to join the founding team of a new private university in Abuja.
	This is an exciting opportunity to shape the visual identity of a world-class institution from the ground up.
	You will be responsible for producing high-quality design assets across print, digital, and environmental media – from brochures and billboards to social media graphics and event backdrops.
	Your work will directly influence how prospective students, parents, donors, and partners perceive the university.
	This is not just a design role; it is a chance to build a brand legacy.


Key Responsibilities
Brand & Creative Development:


	Develop and maintain the university’s visual identity (logos, colour palettes, typography, imagery guidelines) in line with the brand strategy.
	Create a comprehensive brand style guide and ensure consistent application across all departments.
	Conceptualise and execute creative campaigns for student recruitment, Open Days, convocation ceremonies, and fundraising events.


Design Production:


	Design print collateral: prospectuses, flyers, posters, banners, brochures, magazines, letterheads, and business cards.
	Design digital assets: website graphics, social media posts (Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook), email newsletters, digital ads, and presentations (PowerPoint/Canva/Google Slides).
	Produce environmental graphics: signage, wayfinding, exhibition stands, pull up banners, and campus interior displays.
	Edit and retouch photographs; source or create illustrations, icons, and infographics.


Collaboration & Project Management:


	Work closely with the Marketing, Academic Affairs, and Student Affairs teams to translate their content into compelling visuals.
	Manage multiple design projects simultaneously, from brief to final delivery, meeting tight deadlines (e.g., accreditation documents, admission cycles).
	Liaise with external printers, sign makers, and vendors to ensure high-quality production within budget.


Digital & Multimedia Support:


	Assist in basic video editing (e.g., short promotional clips for social media) and animation (motion graphics) when required.
	Ensure all designs are optimised for web and mobile (file size, resolution, accessibility).
	Brand Compliance
	Review all departmental materials to ensure visual consistency and brand integrity.
	Maintain an organised digital asset library (logos, photos, templates) accessible to authorised staff.


Qualifications & Experience
Essential:


	Bachelor’s degree or Higher National Diploma (HND) in Graphic Design, Visual Arts, Fine Arts, Communication Design, or a closely related field.
	Minimum of 2–5 years of professional graphic design experience in a corporate, agency, or institutional setting (experience in education, publishing, or non-profit sectors is a strong plus).


A strong portfolio demonstrating:


	Brand identity projects
	Print and digital collateral
	Typography and layout skills
	Understanding of colour theory and composition
	Proven proficiency in industry-standard software: Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) and optionally Adobe Premiere Pro / After Effects.
	Experience preparing print-ready files and liaising with commercial printers.


Salary & Benefits


	A monthly salary of N200,000 - N300,000 monthly depending on experience.
	Access to university facilities (library, sports, etc.)
	Opportunities for professional development (training, conferences, software subscriptions).


Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send a detailed CV to: [email protected] using “Graphic Designer – [Your Full Name]” as the subject of the email.

Note


	A cover letter (1–2 pages) addressing:
	Your design philosophy and approach to brand building
	A specific example of a design project that improved brand perception or engagement metrics
	A link to your online portfolio (Behance, Dribbble, personal website) or attach a PDF portfolio showing 5–12 of your best projects with brief descriptions.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

title: Finance Manager Job at Primestix Construction Limited

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance Manager",
  "description": "At Primestix Construction we are committed to revolutionizing the construction industry through unparalleled design, advanced automation, and innovative practices. We strive to exceed expectations and set new standards for quality and sustainability

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location: Abuja
Job type: Full-time (Onsite)

Job Description


	We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Finance Manager to provide strong financial leadership across budgeting, reporting, compliance, and cost control functions within our projects and operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Financial reporting, analysis, and performance tracking
	Budget preparation, forecasting, and cash flow management
	Project cost control and financial monitoring
	Tax, audit, and statutory compliance management
	Payroll, vendor payments, and finance operations oversight
	Support strategic financial planning and decision-making


Requirements


	B.Sc/HND in Accounting, Finance, or related field with 3&ndash;5 years of relevant finance/accounting experience
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting and taxation
	Proficiency in Excel and accounting/ERP systems
	ICAN/ACCA qualification is an added advantage
	Experience in construction/real estate preferred


Core Competencies:


	Financial Acumen
	Budgeting
	Cost Control
	Compliance
	Analytical Thinking
	Reporting Accuracy


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: careers@primestixng.com using the Job Position as subject of email.",

Finance Manager Job at Primestix Construction Limited




        
    

        
        At Primestix Construction we are committed to revolutionizing the construction industry through unparalleled design, advanced automation, and innovative practices. We strive to exceed expectations and set new standards for quality and sustainability

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance Manager

Job Location: Abuja
Job type: Full-time (Onsite)

Job Description
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a highly analytical and detail-oriented Finance Manager to provide strong financial leadership across budgeting, reporting, compliance, and cost control functions within our projects and operations.


Key Responsibilities


	Financial reporting, analysis, and performance tracking
	Budget preparation, forecasting, and cash flow management
	Project cost control and financial monitoring
	Tax, audit, and statutory compliance management
	Payroll, vendor payments, and finance operations oversight
	Support strategic financial planning and decision-making


Requirements


	B.Sc/HND in Accounting, Finance, or related field with 3–5 years of relevant finance/accounting experience
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting and taxation
	Proficiency in Excel and accounting/ERP systems
	ICAN/ACCA qualification is an added advantage
	Experience in construction/real estate preferred


Core Competencies:


	Financial Acumen
	Budgeting
	Cost Control
	Compliance
	Analytical Thinking
	Reporting Accuracy


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should forward their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as subject of email.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Sales Assistant",
  "description": "Nemitt Pharmaceuticals Limited is an indigenous pharmaceutical company incorporated in July 1988 as a fully capitalized limited liability company with RC no 116 088 to among other activities, engage in the wholesale of Pharmaceutical products. Nemitt Pharmaceuticals Limited was born out of the dire need for an alternative to the hitherto existing chaotic distribution in the pharmaceutical supply chain. with the goal of addressing the gaps in the distribution value chain.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Assistant
Job Location: Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	Are you passionate about customer service, sales support, and building strong client relationships? We are looking for a proactive and detail-oriented Sales Assistant to support our growing sales operations and help deliver excellent service to our customers.


Key Responsibilities


	Support the sales team with order processing and customer follow-up
	Attend to customer inquiries and provide product information
	Assist with invoice preparation and sales documentation
	Maintain proper customer records and sales reports
	Coordinate with warehouse and logistics teams for timely delivery
	Monitor stock availability and communicate updates to customers
	Support relationship-building with pharmacies and retail customers
	Assist in achieving sales targets and improving customer satisfaction


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND/B.Sc. in any related field
	Previous experience in sales, customer service, or pharmaceutical support is an advantage
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Good organizational and multitasking ability
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
	Positive attitude and willingness to learn
	Candididate my reside close to Ojuelegba.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: hrnemitt@gmail.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

Sales Assistant Job at Nemitt Pharmaceuticals Limited




        
    

        
        Nemitt Pharmaceuticals Limited is an indigenous pharmaceutical company incorporated in July 1988 as a fully capitalized limited liability company with RC no 116 088 to among other activities, engage in the wholesale of Pharmaceutical products. Nemitt Pharmaceuticals Limited was born out of the dire need for an alternative to the hitherto existing chaotic distribution in the pharmaceutical supply chain. with the goal of addressing the gaps in the distribution value chain.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Sales Assistant
Job Location: Surulere, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Are you passionate about customer service, sales support, and building strong client relationships? We are looking for a proactive and detail-oriented Sales Assistant to support our growing sales operations and help deliver excellent service to our customers.


Key Responsibilities


	Support the sales team with order processing and customer follow-up
	Attend to customer inquiries and provide product information
	Assist with invoice preparation and sales documentation
	Maintain proper customer records and sales reports
	Coordinate with warehouse and logistics teams for timely delivery
	Monitor stock availability and communicate updates to customers
	Support relationship-building with pharmacies and retail customers
	Assist in achieving sales targets and improving customer satisfaction


Requirements


	Minimum of OND/HND/B.Sc. in any related field
	Previous experience in sales, customer service, or pharmaceutical support is an advantage
	Strong communication and interpersonal skills
	Good organizational and multitasking ability
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office tools
	Ability to work in a fast-paced environment
	Positive attitude and willingness to learn
	Candididate my reside close to Ojuelegba.


Salary
N100,000 - N150,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Software Engineer - Backend",
  "description": "Cowrywise Financial Technology Limited is a digital wealth management platform democratizing access to savings and investment products for the underserved but growing Millenials in Africa. Joining us would mean being part of an interdisciplinary team dedicated to a lofty vision of building the next-generation wealth management platform for Africans.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Software Engineer - Backend

Job Location: Lagos

The role


	We need a backend engineer who can own features end-to-end, from database schema to production.
	You&rsquo;ll work on our core financial platform: portfolios, transactions, third-party integrations, and the systems that keep everything running reliably.
	This role is highly collaborative. You&rsquo;ll work closely with product and design to figure out what we&rsquo;re building, then take ownership of how it gets built and shipped.
	Your code handles real money, so correctness matters. We also ship frequently, so speed matters too. The best candidates know how to balance both.


What you&rsquo;ll do
Features:


	Build backend features for savings products, portfolios, and stock investments
	Own the whole thing: database design, business logic, APIs, tests, deployment, monitoring
	Write code that someone else can read and maintain a year from now
	Work with frontend/mobile to define API contracts.


Systems:


	Build integrations with payment gateways (Paystack, Monnify, etc), fund managers, and KYC providers

Software Engineer - Backend Job at Cowrywise Financial Technology Limited




        
    

        
        Cowrywise Financial Technology Limited is a digital wealth management platform democratizing access to savings and investment products for the underserved but growing Millenials in Africa. Joining us would mean being part of an interdisciplinary team dedicated to a lofty vision of building the next-generation wealth management platform for Africans.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Software Engineer - Backend

Job Location: Lagos

The role

	We need a backend engineer who can own features end-to-end, from database schema to production.
	You’ll work on our core financial platform: portfolios, transactions, third-party integrations, and the systems that keep everything running reliably.
	This role is highly collaborative. You’ll work closely with product and design to figure out what we’re building, then take ownership of how it gets built and shipped.
	Your code handles real money, so correctness matters. We also ship frequently, so speed matters too. The best candidates know how to balance both.


What you’ll do
Features:


	Build backend features for savings products, portfolios, and stock investments
	Own the whole thing: database design, business logic, APIs, tests, deployment, monitoring
	Write code that someone else can read and maintain a year from now
	Work with frontend/mobile to define API contracts.


Systems:


	Build integrations with payment gateways (Paystack, Monnify, etc), fund managers, and KYC providers
	Design systems that handle money correctly. Idempotency, consistency, audit trails. Non-negotiable.
	Debug production issues and be on call for the systems you build.


Performance:


	Optimize database queries, design schemas, and make things fast
	PostgreSQL and Redis for the data layer
	Celery for background jobs.


Code quality:


	Review code and get your code reviewed
	Write tests that actually catch bugs
	Help onboard new engineers.


What we’re looking for
Required:


	5+ years building production backend systems
	Our primary language is Python (Django/FastAPI), but proficiency in Golang and JavaScript (and TypeScript) is also important
	PostgreSQL, MYSQL: schema design, query optimization, transactions
	Event-driven architectures, message queues (SQS, RabbitMQ, Kafka)
	REST APIs: clean contracts, versioning, error handling
	AWS basics (EC2, RDS, S3, SQS, Lambda)
	Background job processing (Celery or similar)
	Can debug through logs, metrics, and code
	Git, CI/CD, modern workflows
	Can explain technical concepts to non-technical people.


Nice to have:


	Fintech, payments, or financial services experience
	Docker Swarm, Kubernetes, ECS
	Monitoring tools (New Relic, Sentry, CloudWatch)
	Typescript and Golang Experience.


The people who succeed on this team:


	Take ownership without being asked
	Know when to optimize and when to ship
	Write code for humans
	Adapt when requirements change
	Give and receive feedback well
	Care about users, not just code
	Make the people around them better.
